seoTitle: How to Pay With PayPal Goods and Services (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to send a protected Goods and Services payment on PayPal to keep purchase protection active when buying from a seller.
intro: Buying from an online seller or freelancer is safer when you use PayPal's Goods and Services option instead of a personal transfer. This guide walks through sending a protected payment and avoiding the common mistake of choosing Friends and Family by accident. It's for anyone who wants purchase protection coverage on a transaction.

steps.heading: Open Send & Request
steps.body: Log in to PayPal and click the Send & Request icon in the top menu.

steps.heading: Enter the recipient's details
steps.body: Carefully type the seller's verified PayPal email address or mobile number.

steps.heading: Enter the amount and currency
steps.body: Input your transfer value and select the correct currency for the transaction.

steps.heading: Select Goods and Services
steps.body: On the payment type screen, explicitly choose Goods and Services to enable purchase protection instead of the personal option.

steps.heading: Add a note and confirm
steps.body: Include item details or an order reference in the note field, verify the fee breakdown, then click Send Payment Now.

faq.question: Why does Goods and Services cost more than Friends and Family?
faq.answer: Sellers typically absorb a standard commercial merchant surcharge in exchange for the buyer protection that comes with this payment type.

faq.question: What happens if an item never arrives?
faq.answer: Goods and Services payments are eligible for PayPal's Purchase Protection, which can help you recover funds through a dispute.

faq.question: Can I switch a payment type after sending it?
faq.answer: No, once sent, the payment type is locked in, so it's important to select the correct option before confirming.

faq.question: Does adding an order note help with disputes?
faq.answer: Yes, clear notes describing the item or service create a useful record if a dispute needs to be filed later.
